rulings.Case Title: KEVIN NEALAN VS. LEDCOR CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 26, 2018, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT LEDCOR CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint Cross-Complainant Ledcor Construction, Inc.'s motion for leave to file first amended cross-complaint is GRANTED. California has a liberal policy of allowing leave to amend and where undue prejudice to opposing parties can be avoided, denial of leave to amend that prevents a meritorious cause of action constitutes an abuse of discretion. (Redevelopment Agency v. Herrold (1978) 86 Cal.App.3d 1024, 1031.) Ledcor's did not unreasonably delay in bringing this motion as it had no reason to sue ACCO prior to the September 28, 2018 announcement that ACCO's insurer would no longer indemnify Ledcor. Moreover, evidence putting Ledcor on notice of ACCO's alleged liability was not discovered until the October 17, 2018 deposition of ACCO employee Michael Castillo. Taking Ledcor's representations that no further discovery will be necessary given the contractual nature of its claims against ACCO as true, there is no prejudice to other parties. Therefore, leave to amend is provided, but discovery will not be reopened. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ledcor is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/NL)
